name: tui-thread-and-logging-integration
description: The Textual TUI runs run_flow_once in a worker thread (it's a blocking sync call that runs the supervisor's own asyncio loop internally, which must not share Textual's loop) and drives its live EventFeed by attaching a logging.Handler to the same JSONL-emitting log path — no changes to orchestrator.py needed. BEEAI_LOG_LEVEL=WARNING is set via os.environ BEFORE any beeai_framework import because BeeAI's Logger attaches a raw stdout StreamHandler at construction that bypasses both configure_logging() and Textual's alternate-screen rendering (stray stdout writes garble the terminal).
invariants: The BEEAI_LOG_LEVEL setdefault must stay above the bee_bug_hunter.* imports — CONFIG.log_level is read once at import time
Anomaly/Results screens read the anomaly/bug_report/perf_report keys of run_flow_once's return dict, sourced via delegation_capture
Every bordered content panel carries the shared '.panel' class; UI colors come from the module-level palette constants, never re-hardcoded hex
contracts: log_ui() routes every screen change / click / keypress into the same JSONL stream as flow-run events
